screenshot_note: Puppeteer unavailable in this environment (pthread_create resource limit). Visual assessment based on code review, HTML structure, and screenshots from review #19 (same codebase, zero visual changes detected).
fixes_since_last_review: NONE. All 10 issues from review #19 remain open. Zero fixes landed.
summary: The office is still visually gorgeous and delightful — vibe system reactive, avatars crisp, confetti still pops on shipping. But this is a full cycle of zero fixes: all 10 bugs from review #19 are still open, and three new technical debt items appeared. stableActivityLog is still a no-op useMemo defeating React.memo on every 30s poll. Luna's gradient ID collision silently corrupts her hair color when she goes idle. The confetti vibe-burst is now the only timer in the app without a cleanup ref — a memory leak waiting to happen. Time-formatting logic now has four independent copies spread across three files, growing worse each sprint. code_quality drops to 2 this round. Fun factor stays locked at 5 because honestly this office is a joy to look at. Needs a dedicated debt sprint before the next feature.

suggestions.title: Luna SVG gradient ID collision when she goes idle (PERSISTS from #19)
suggestions.description: CharacterAvatar builds gradId as `luna-hair-${workerId}` which always yields 'luna-hair-luna'. When Luna is idle, she renders twice simultaneously: once as a wandering Character and once as a mini-avatar inside ConferenceTable (meetingWorkers requires >=2 idle workers). Two SVG elements in the same document sharing the same gradient ID means the second SVG picks up the wrong gradient definition, rendering Luna with broken or missing hair color in the conference table mini-avatar. Fix: pass a unique instanceId prop to CharacterAvatar and append it to gradId, e.g. `luna-hair-luna-desk` vs `luna-hair-luna-conf`. The simplest approach is a module-level counter: let _gradIdx = 0 at module scope, then inside the component const gradId = useRef(`luna-hair-${workerId}-${_gradIdx++}`).current.

suggestions.title: stableActivityLog no-op useMemo defeats React.memo on WorkerCard (PERSISTS from #19)
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 772: `const stableActivityLog = useMemo(() => activityLog, [activityLog])` is functionally identical to writing `const stableActivityLog = activityLog`. The memo returns a new reference every time activityLog changes (every 30s poll), which passes a new activityEntries prop to every WorkerCard and defeats React.memo entirely — ALL worker cards re-render every 30s even when their individual data is unchanged. Fix: slice per-worker activity entries before WorkerCard receives them, e.g. compute a filtered subset in the map callback and pass only the worker's slice. Or add a custom comparator to React.memo that does a shallow equality check on the filtered slice. Either way, delete the stableActivityLog line — it is dead code that misleads.

suggestions.title: Confetti vibe-burst setTimeout has no cleanup ref — stale state write on fast vibe change
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 864-865: `setConfettiActive(true); setTimeout(() => setConfettiActive(false), 2500)` is the only timer in the entire app that does NOT store its ID in a ref. Every other timeout (workerConfettiTimerRef, completionToastTimerRef, doorEventTimerRef, newClearTimersRef, etc.) is properly tracked and cancelled. If teamVibe.key changes twice within 2.5s, the first stale timeout fires mid-second-confetti and kills it early. If the component unmounts during the confetti window, the callback still fires and calls setState on a dead component. Fix: add `const confettiTimerRef = useRef(null)` and replace the raw setTimeout with the same pattern used everywhere else: `if (confettiTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(confettiTimerRef.current); confettiTimerRef.current = setTimeout(() => { setConfettiActive(false); confettiTimerRef.current = null }, 2500)`.

suggestions.title: Four independent time-formatting functions — extract to utils/time.js (PERSISTS from #19, got worse)
suggestions.description: The codebase now has four separate time-formatting implementations: (1) getRelativeTime() App.jsx:28 — 'Xm ago' / 'Xh ago' / 'Xd ago'. (2) formatDuration() App.jsx:215 — 'Working for Xh Ym'. (3) formatIdleDuration() Office.jsx:318 — bare 'Xh Ym'. (4) Inline IIFE in ghost bubble Office.jsx:540-548 — exact duplicate of getRelativeTime. Plus a fifth partial copy in ghostDeskTitle Office.jsx:1050-1052. This was two duplicates in review #19; it is now four-plus. Fix: create src/utils/time.js with formatElapsed(ts) and getRelativeTime(ts), import from all call sites. Remove the inline IIFE and ghostDeskTitle inline code. Dead code elimination + single source of truth.

suggestions.title: Hover card shows time-since-last-fetch, not time-on-task, for working workers (PERSISTS from #19)
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 594: the char hover card renders `formatIdleDuration(worker.updated_at)` for ALL variants including 'working'. For working workers, updated_at is the last data-refresh timestamp, not when the task started. A worker who began a task 3h ago but whose data refreshed 2m ago shows '⏱ 2m' — actively misleading. The workers.json schema includes startedAt. Fix: for variant==='working', use formatIdleDuration(worker.startedAt || worker.updated_at) with an explicit 'on task for' label. For variant==='idle', keep updated_at with an 'idle for' label. Replace the ambiguous clock emoji-only label with visible text so the context is unambiguous.

suggestions.title: completionToast and plantToast use 15-property inline style objects (PERSISTS from #19)
suggestions.description: App.jsx:969-992 and Office.jsx:958-982 each inline a massive style object (position, bottom, left, transform, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, pointerEvents, animation). These objects recreate on every parent render, force style recalculation, and are unreadable. Flagged in #19, still unfixed. Fix: add .completion-toast and .plant-toast classes in the respective CSS files. Use a single inline style for the one dynamic property: `style={{ '--toast-color': completionToast.color }}` and reference it in CSS as `border-color: var(--toast-color); box-shadow: 0 0 12px var(--toast-color);`.

suggestions.title: No React ErrorBoundary — any render crash shows a blank screen (PERSISTS from #19)
suggestions.description: There is no ErrorBoundary anywhere in the component tree. A runtime error in CharacterAvatar, WorkerCard, or ConferenceTable unmounts the entire application. The app fetches live external JSON every 30s — unexpected worker shapes, null fields, or malformed data are plausible failure modes. With 92 keyframe animations in Office.css and complex SVG rendering, a one-off bad datum could brick the UI. Fix: create a minimal ErrorBoundary class component and wrap <Office>, the dashboard workers grid, and the <App> root in separate boundaries so a crash in one area degrades gracefully without killing the whole page.

suggestions.title: activityLogLenRef is dead code — created and updated but never read
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 771: `const activityLogLenRef = useRef(0)` and line 773: `useEffect(() => { activityLogLenRef.current = activityLog.length }, [activityLog.length])`. This ref is written but never read anywhere in the component. It has no effect on rendering or behavior. The accompanying useEffect runs on every activityLog.length change for no reason. Fix: delete both lines. If per-entry diffs were intended here, document the intent before the next engineer wastes time searching for the read site.

suggestions.title: Activity filter buttons use role='radio' on <button> elements (PERSISTS from #19)
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1159-1196: both the type filter and worker filter bars place role='radio' on <button> elements inside role='radiogroup' containers. While not invalid, it creates confusing AT announcements — screen readers call these 'radio buttons' but they look and behave like toggle buttons, and keyboard navigation within the group may not follow expected radio button patterns. Fix option A (correct): replace with actual <input type='radio'> elements styled as pills. Fix option B (pragmatic): remove role='radiogroup'/'radio' and replace aria-checked with aria-pressed, turning them into a standard toolbar toggle group. Option B is lower effort and semantically more honest.

suggestions.title: IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS defined inside Character component body (PERSISTS from #19)
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 390: `const IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS = { 1: '☕', 2: '💬', 3: '🪟', 4: '🧘' }` is declared inside the Character memo function body. This allocates a new object on every render of every Character instance. Since Character is memo-wrapped the frequency is low, but it violates the convention established by the rest of the file (AWAY_MESSAGES and DESK_SCREEN_SVG are module-level constants). Move IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS to module scope alongside its siblings.
